scenario_results.name: Hybrid demand intake with authoritative record mapping
covered_families: normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires CMDB, dependency-map, RTO/RPO, change-calendar, and cloud estate records before producing recommendation-only architecture guidance.

scenario_results.name: Demand exceeds available hybrid capacity
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario produces a capacity-aware constraint log and escalates when private connectivity, backup windows, staffing, or maintenance-window capacity is insufficient.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break
covered_families: conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario blocks deterministic migration sequencing until an approved priority policy or decision owner is supplied.

scenario_results.name: Hybrid topology architecture recommendation
covered_families: normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario tests topology guidance using landing zones, VMware, Azure, AWS, workload criticality, data residency, connectivity, and control-plane ownership.

scenario_results.name: Migration wave plan and validation checklist
covered_families: phased migration plan and validation checklist
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates phased planning with dependency order, pilot scope, rollback triggers, validation checkpoints, and cutover approval boundaries.

scenario_results.name: Networking and segmentation reference design
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers private connectivity, VPN failover, SD-WAN, DNS, segmentation, failure domains, and network/security handoffs.

scenario_results.name: Hybrid DR failover runbook
covered_families: runbooks, escalation paths, and rollback strategy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires RTO/RPO, recovery dependencies, test evidence, failover sequence, rollback criteria, and continuity escalation.

scenario_results.name: Observability and incident posture
covered_families: operational posture summary with monitoring and logging plan
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates incident triage across cloud metrics, on-prem logs, DNS, route, SD-WAN, change, and incident records.

scenario_results.name: Shared egress cost, reliability, and isolation tradeoff
covered_families: cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario compares cost drivers, reliability, isolation, and blast radius while refusing pricing guarantees or approval claims.

scenario_results.name: Industry-specific prerequisite and evidence gap
covered_families: low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
do not treat industry name as cosmetic
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects generic hybrid-cloud guidance when workload inventory, topology, RTO/RPO, classification, and cloud account evidence are missing.

scenario_results.name: Application code refactor refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario clearly refuses application code, framework migration, and CI/CD implementation and routes to application/platform engineering.

scenario_results.name: Primary cloud identity policy refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects primary identity policy ownership and routes to identity/cloud identity specialists.

scenario_results.name: Commercial pricing and licensing refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses pricing guarantees, contract law interpretation, and licensing audit decisions while naming FinOps/procurement/legal owners.

scenario_results.name: Endpoint and SaaS administration refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses endpoint device and pure SaaS administration ownership and routes to adjacent specialists.

scenario_results.name: Adjacent cloud security and FinOps route
covered_families: request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario routes security exception approval and reserved-capacity purchase decisions to cloud security, FinOps, procurement, and human approval owners.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting policy and final approval escalation
covered_families: request for final approval outside lane authority
escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses production cutover approval, treats tenant CAB policy as a controlling constraint, and escalates the policy conflict.

scenario_results.name: specification-based materialization and no-regression gate
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario preserves specification-based runtime posture, refuses retained trusted implementation ownership without promotion evidence, and blocks release on unreviewed regressions.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with source freshness
covered_families: tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario adapts Azure Arc, AWS hybrid connectivity, Google Cloud analytics, and Terraform provider evidence to tenant policy while preserving public-source baseline and escalation rules.

notes: Scenario pass count is 18/18, exceeding the minimum of 10 scenarios and 0.90 pass rate.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, the planning-scheduling-and-allocation workflow phase, and the cross-industry profile.
Research summary satisfies the 2026-04-08 constrained research contract with no blocking unresolved ambiguity.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based; no retained implementation ownership is claimed.
